description:
`bl prime --json` prints the literal string `sync complete` to stdout before the JSON document, breaking naive `bl prime --json | jq` / `python3 -c 'json.load(...)'` pipelines.

Source: src/commands/sync.rs:26 — `println!("sync complete")` in cmd_sync. cmd_prime calls cmd_sync unconditionally, so the line lands on stdout regardless of --json.

Skill currently promises (in "Scripting with bl"): "These commands accept --json and print a single JSON document to stdout (stderr is diagnostics only)". This bug violates that contract.

bl ready --json and bl list --json are clean — bug is specific to prime because of the embedded sync call.

Fix: switch to eprintln!, or suppress the line when cmd_sync is invoked from cmd_prime with json=true. Test: `bl prime --json | python3 -c 'import sys,json; json.load(sys.stdin)'` should succeed.
title: prime --json: 'sync complete' leaks to stdout
